_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 TX state legislators: Bryan Hughes (SD1), Gary VanDeaver (HD1), Bob Hall (SD2), Brent Money (HD2), Cecil Bell Jr (HD3). Cross-party catches: VanDeaver anti-voucher + fiscal F (lame-duck, not running); Bell lost 2026 GOP primary.

bryan-hughes@TX.notes_set: TX SD-1 senator since 2017; Chair of State Affairs, widely ranked the most conservative TX senator. Authored SB8 Heartbeat Act (2021), SB1 omnibus election-integrity law (2021), the 'In God We Trust' school-display law, SB559 religious-conscience/free-speech protections, SB1257 (gender-transition de-transition coverage), SB1072 (LGBTQ classroom-content restrictions), SB2334 (state gold-backed digital currency). TFR Fiscal Index A (2025), 91/100 tied #1 (2023). Texas Right to Life Horizon Award. No cross-party deviation found. Cells without a clean cited source (con-carry vote, anti-red-flag SB1362, public-justice items, border roll-calls) left null pending vote-history confirmation.

profile.confidence_note: Lame-duck: announced 2025-12-01 he will NOT seek reelection but serves through Jan 2027. Web-verified roster + voucher votes + ratings.

gary-vandeaver@TX.notes_set: TX HD-1 rep; rural establishment Republican. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: one of only two House Republicans to vote NO on the 2025 ESA/private-school voucher bill (also voted no 2023), citing protection of small rural districts; drew an Abbott-backed primary challenge and an Oct 2025 RPT censure. Fiscal record weak: TFR career grade 'F'. Socially conservative on life (Texas Alliance for Life 89th-session scorecard 97%) and family/marriage (Texas Values Action Faith&Family 86%); NRA 'A' rating + 2024 NRA-PVF endorsement, voted for constitutional/open/campus carry. Announced 2025-12-01 he will not seek reelection; serves through Jan 2027.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 89th-session scorecard 97% (voted for Life of the Mother Act, abortion-travel ban, mail-order abortion-drug enforcement).

1.note: Not an abolition maximalist; censured for stalling HB1304 (fetal-tissue labeling) in his committee. Not scored TRUE on abolition.

bob-hall@TX.notes_set: TX SD-2 senator since 2015; among the most conservative members (YCT 92, Texas Right to Life 100, Texas Eagle Forum 95, Texas Values Action 98, TFR Fiscal Index A). Signature issue: election integrity — authored SB77 (89th) mandating hand-marked paper ballots and a return to precinct-based voting, plus a tabulator data-storage ban (88th). Co-sponsored SB15 'Save Women's Sports Act'; authored SB89 (material harmful to minors). Voted for SB2 ESA/school-choice ('proud to vote for it'). Instrumental in TX Second Amendment Sanctuary State effort. No cross-party deviation found (his frictions run to the right of leadership).

profile.confidence_note: First full session (89th, 2025); sworn in Jan 2025, running for reelection Nov 2026. Some cells rest on cited self-statements.

brent-money@TX.notes_set: TX HD-2 rep (Hunt/Hopkins/Van Zandt); newer hard-right member, sworn in Jan 2025. Authored HB2197 (treat abortion as criminal homicide — abolitionist); Texas Alliance for Life 89th scorecard 95%. Sponsored ban on gender-transition care for all ages. Voted YES on SB2 voucher/ESA. Founded the 'Sharia-Free Texas Caucus'; publicly advocated Christian governance ('best ruled by Christian men'). TFR 2025 Fiscal Index 98 (Pro-Taxpayer). Opposes foreign/Chinese ownership of TX farmland. NOTE: voted 'Present Not Voting' on HB7 (mail-order abortion-drug enforcement) as a RIGHT-flank protest (viewed it as insufficiently abolitionist) — NOT a pro-choice move; not scored against life.

profile.confidence_note: Lost the March 3, 2026 GOP primary to Kristen Plaisance (55.1%-44.9%); lame-duck through Jan 2027. Web-verified.

cecil-bell-jr@TX.notes_set: TX HD-3 rep since 2013. CURRENCY FIX: LOST the March 3, 2026 GOP primary to Tim Dunn-backed Kristen Plaisance (55.1%-44.9%, no runoff) — not a 2026 ballot contender; serves through Jan 2027. Authored HB623 'Texas Preservation of Sovereignty and Marriage Act' (2015, blocking same-sex marriage recognition) and a 2017 federal-nullification proposal. Voted for SB8 (2021) and HB1927 constitutional carry; self-reports A+ NRA/TSRA. Establishment-leaning intra-party signals (voted for Burrows as Speaker, against Paxton impeachment) and a weak TFR fiscal record (2025 index 63; historical 'F' grades) — fiscal deviation flagged.
